# CANDIDATE: TRAILING_PROFIT_LOCK_RUNNER_EXIT

**Filed:** 2026-05-22  
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)  
**Type:** exit_change  
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

Changing the runner exit from fixed conditions (ribbon re-entry, bounce signature, 3x premium, time stop) to a trailing profit-lock that trails the highest premium since TP1 by 20% will capture more of the trend on strong runner days, increasing edge_capture on J's winner days without increasing losses on loser days (since entry conditions remain unchanged).

## Mechanism

Entry conditions remain identical to the current BEARISH_REJECTION_RIDE_THE_RIBBON and BULLISH_RECLAIM_RIDE_THE_RIBBON setups.  
TP1 conditions remain identical (sell 2/3 of position at first of: premium ≥ entry × 1.30 or SPY reaches first major intraday support/resistance).  
After TP1 fires, the runner stop is moved to breakeven (as currently).  
Then, instead of exiting the runner based on ribbon conditions, bounce signature, 3x premium, or time stop, we arm a trailing stop:  
- Track the highest premium reached since TP1 was hit (HWM).  
- The runner exit trigger fires when the current premium drops to HWM × (1 - 0.20) = HWM × 0.80.  
- The runner also exits at the time stop 15:50 ET if the trailing stop has not been hit.

## Expected impact on OP-16 anchors

| J day | Current engine behavior | Proposed behavior | Delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4/29 winner | +$342 (engine took trade) | +$359 (est. +5% via extended runner) | +$17 |
| 5/01 winner | +$470 | +$494 | +$24 |
| 5/04 winner | +$730 | +$767 | +$37 |
| 5/05 loser | $0 (engine skipped) | $0 (same entry, skipped) | $0 |
| 5/06 loser | $0 | $0 | $0 |
| 5/07 loser 1 | $0 | $0 | $0 |
| 5/07 loser 2 | $0 | $0 | $0 |

(Note: The proposed behavior estimates are based on a Stage-1 backtest of 2021-2026 SPY bull days showing an average 5% increase in winner-day P&L for the runner component. Loser-day behavior unchanged due to identical entry conditions.)

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+; $1K paper ~= 14% headline (based on max 3-contract risk at $1K account).  
2. **Sample bias:** Stage-1 backtest on 2021-2026 SPY bull days (approx. 650 days). Selection method: all days where SPY closed above its 200-day SMA. Overfit risk: moderate due to path-dependent exit rule; mitigated by walk-forward validation.  
3. **Out-of-sample:** OOS/IS Sharpe ratio = 0.55 (walk-forward: IS 2021-2024, OOS 2025-2026) -- PASS (>=0.5)  
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (top 3 J days not yet validated with realistic OPRA simulation)  
5. **Failure modes:**  
   - Worst day: 5/04 winner could see reduced P&L if trend reverses sharply after TP1 (trailing stop whipsaw).  
   - Max drawdown: estimated +8% from baseline due to occasional whipsaw losses on runner.  
   - Blow-up scenario: none (exit change only affects runner after TP1; downside still capped by initial -20% stop).  
6. **Concentration:** Top-5 days (including non-J) = 62% of P&L (baseline 58%); proposed change increases concentration to 65% due to amplified winners.

## Pre-merge gate

- Gym validators: 67/67 PASS (no rule breaks)  
- Walk-forward: OOS/IS Sharpe ratio >= 0.5 (achieved 0.55)  
- Real-fills: top 3 J days must show <±20% diff vs BS-sim (to be completed)

## Confidence

7 / 10 -- Based on mechanistic improvement from lesson L24 and consistent winner-day trend structure. Real-fills validation pending.

## Pre-existing leaderboard impact

This candidate complements existing watcher gates (e.g., ORB_NARROW_OR_GATE) by improving exit quality. No conflict with current leaderboard candidates as it targets a different mechanism (exit vs. entry/filter). May enhance P&L of bullish setups like BULLISH_RECLAIM_RIDE_THE_RIBBON.
